benchmark_details.name: WinoQueer
benchmark_details.overview: To measure anti-queer bias, we introduce a new benchmark dataset, WinoQueer, modeled after other bias-detection benchmarks but addressing homophobic and transphobic biases.
benchmark_details.data_type: text (template-based paired sentence examples for masked language modeling / pseudo-log-likelihood evaluation)

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: Quantify language model biases against specific subpopulations of the LGBTQ+ community (measure homophobic and transphobic biases) and evaluate mitigation via fine-tuning on data written by and/or about queer people.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: This work is still ongoing, and we plan to develop an even more complete version of WinoQueer in the future. Our methodology is far from perfect, and may have the unintended consequence of silencing some community members.

data.source: QueerTwitter: Tweets collected via the Twitter Academic API using anti-trans bill numbers and hashtags (iterative co-occurring hashtag expansion). QueerNews: US national news articles collected via Media Cloud using keywords related to anti-trans bills and LGBTQ+ identity.
data.annotation: Automatically generated from template schemas (template-based paired sentence generation with pronoun and name slotting); no manual annotation procedure described.

methods: Masked Language Modeling evaluation using pseudo-log-likelihood metric (paired stereotypical vs. less-stereotypical sentences)
Coreference resolution evaluation on OntoNotes and GAP datasets
Model fine-tuning on corpora (QueerTwitter, QueerNews) and evaluation of off-the-shelf vs. fine-tuned models
metrics: Bias score (percentage of examples where the more stereotypical sentence has higher pseudo-log-likelihood)
Bias ratio for GAP (ratio of F1 for she/her pronouns to F1 for he/him pronouns)
methodology.calculation: Bias score: percent of examples for which the likelihood of the more stereotypical sentence is higher than the likelihood of the less stereotypical sentence. A perfect score is 0.5. F1 and bias ratio computed as standard (ratio of F1 for she/her to F1 for he/him for GAP).
methodology.interpretation: Bias score > 0.5 indicates preference for more stereotypical (straight) sentences (presence of homophobic bias); a perfect unbiased score is 0.5. For GAP bias ratio, a perfectly unbiased model would have a bias score of 1. Higher F1 indicates better coreference resolution performance.
methodology.baseline_results: Masked LM bias score (percent straight > gay): Table 2 - BERT_base: Off-the-shelf 73.77, Finetune QueerNews 57.32, Finetune QueerTwitter 55.33. BERT_large: Off-the-shelf 76.33, Finetune QueerNews 70.3, Finetune QueerTwitter 71.76. SpanBERT_base: Off-the-shelf 46.0, Finetune QueerNews 50.61, Finetune QueerTwitter 49.64. SpanBERT_large: Off-the-shelf 48.37, Finetune QueerNews 56.28, Finetune QueerTwitter 44.04. OntoNotes F1 (Table 3) and GAP F1/bias (Table 4) reported to demonstrate downstream performance (examples: OntoNotes BERT_base Published 73.9 / Vanilla 74.1 / QueerNews 73.3 / QueerTwitter 72.3; GAP example: BERT_base Vanilla F1 82.4 Bias 0.97).
methodology.validation: Validated by evaluating models on established downstream tasks/datasets (OntoNotes coreference test set and GAP) and comparing to previously published results to ensure fine-tuning does not cause catastrophic forgetting.

targeted_risks.demographic_analysis: Benchmark designed to quantify bias against specific subpopulations of the LGBTQ+ community (e.g., lesbophobia, biphobia, transmisogyny) and explicitly includes they/them and neopronouns in templates.
harm: Potential silencing of community members due to data filtering choices
